summary_one_line: Input must select Sinhala script and place a glyph with codepoint 0x0D85 (bytes 85 0d) so the per-script switch hits case 0x0D85u.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A buffer whose script resolves to HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA and that contains a glyph whose codepoint == 0x0D85 (little-endian bytes 85 0d) at a position where buffer->idx + 1 < count, so the inner per-glyph switch reaches the true side of case 0x0D85u at line 295.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All five winner-resolving seeds carry the repeated 4-byte pattern 85 0d 00 00 (codepoint 0x0D85), and the per-role overlay marks line 295 case 0x0D85u as [W] winner-resolving only; the winner takes the true side 15 times (W T=15) at the blocker line.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry sibling Sinhala codepoints (91 0d = 0x0D91, ca 0d) but never 0x0D85, so the inner switch lands on case 0x0D91u (marked [B], both hit) and the blocker case 0x0D85u stays at T=0; the byte diff shows offset 0x0000 W=85 vs L=91/ca.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: naive_ctx installs a CtxHook that folds the call-context hash into the edge-map index, so reaching the deeply nested per-glyph switch arm via the Sinhala for-loop registers as fresh (call-context, edge) coverage that bare naive's flat edge map already considers seen; this extra granularity rewards the corpus growth that drives the buffer toward a 0x0D85 glyph, resolving the branch in 10/10 trials vs 1/10 for naive.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the true side of case 0x0D85u inside the HB_SCRIPT_SINHALA per-glyph switch.
The blocker case 0x0D85u sits inside the SINHALA script arm reached via the outer script switch.
The per-role overlay marks line 295 case 0x0D85u as winner-resolving only.
Winner-resolving seeds contain the codepoint 0x0D85 as little-endian bytes 85 0d 00 00.
Loser-blocking seeds carry sibling Sinhala codepoint 0x0D91 (bytes 91 0d) rather than 0x0D85.
At offset 0x0000 the winner bytes are dominated by 85 while losers carry 91/ca/4c/0c.
The winner takes the true side of the blocker 15 times while the loser never does (T=0).
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 295  T=15 F=90  T=0 F=90  case 0x0D85u:  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The loser instead lands on the sibling Sinhala arm case 0x0D91u, which both roles hit.
naive_ctx folds a call-context hash into the edge-map index so the same edge is recorded at different map slots per call path.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
folds it into the edge-map index, so the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx feedback treats a new (call-context, edge) pair as new coverage, distinguishing it from bare naive.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: naive_ctx wins the decisive pair 10/10 resolved vs naive 1/10, delta ctx_coverage.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage/dash-row check does not apply; the naive_ctx mechanism block states there are no ParentInfo-only / dash rows for this fuzzer and the winning seeds' mutation_op chains are pure havoc/token (e.g. WordInterestingMutator, BytesSwapMutator, TokenReplace), consistent with a coverage-granularity divergence rather than I2S substitution.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic Sinhala-script harness with a deeply nested per-glyph switch where naive and naive_ctx reach the 0x0D85 case-arm equally fast (no resolved-trial advantage for naive_ctx), showing the context-indexed coverage gave no exploration benefit.

weakest_evidence_point: The causal link between context-sensitive coverage granularity and the corpus actually producing a 0x0D85 glyph is inferred; the prompt shows the byte/coverage correlation but not a direct trace of CtxHook-driven corpus adds leading to that codepoint.
